gaps: No test covers the scenario where deleting a disconnected device causes the remaining device's name to display incorrectly (e.g., showing the deleted device's name or a corrupted name).

No test covers the scenario where deleting a disconnected device causes the remaining device's connection status to be incorrect (e.g., showing disconnected when it's actually connected, or vice versa).
No test covers the scenario where deleting a disconnected device blocks access to the remaining device's settings (tapping the device does not open Device Settings).
No test covers the post-deletion UI state of the remaining device after removing a disconnected device from a multi-device list.
No test covers the specific sequence: turn off Device A -> select disconnected Device A -> delete Device A -> observe remaining Device B's name, status, and settings accessibility.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DELETE-001: Verify remaining device name is correct after deleting a disconnected device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
Ensure two Poly devices are paired and connected: Device A (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20') and Device B (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20+').
Verify both devices appear in the device list with correct names and 'Connected' status.
Turn off Device A using the power button (press for 2 seconds).
Wait 10 seconds for the app to update. Observe Device A shows 'Disconnected' status.
Tap on the disconnected Device A entry in the device list.
Tap the gear icon or menu (three dots) next to Device A's name.
Select 'Delete Device' or 'Remove Device' from the menu.
Confirm deletion when prompted.
Observe the remaining Device B in the device list.
Verify Device B's name is displayed correctly as 'Poly Sync 20+' (or its original paired name, not corrupted and not showing Device A's name).
generated_testcases.expected: After deleting disconnected Device A, the remaining Device B displays its correct original name without corruption or substitution.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DELETE-002: Verify remaining device connection status is correct after deleting a disconnected device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
Pair and connect two devices: Device A (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20') and Device B (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20+').
Confirm both show 'Connected' status in the device list.
Turn off Device A. Wait for status to change to 'Disconnected'.
Tap on Device A (disconnected) in the device list.
Navigate to device options and select 'Delete Device'. Confirm deletion.
Observe Device B in the device list.
Verify Device B shows 'Connected' status (not 'Disconnected', 'Unknown', or blank).
Verify the connection indicator icon (e.g., Bluetooth icon) next to Device B is solid/active, not greyed out.
generated_testcases.expected: After deleting disconnected Device A, the remaining Device B correctly shows 'Connected' status and a solid connection indicator.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DELETE-003: Verify remaining device settings remain accessible after deleting a disconnected device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
Pair and connect two devices: Device A and Device B.
Turn off Device A. Wait for 'Disconnected' status.
Tap on Device A (disconnected) in the device list.
Delete Device A via the device options menu and confirm.
After deletion, tap on Device B's entry in the device list.
Verify the app navigates to Device B's Device Settings screen (showing options like Audio Settings, Firmware Update, Device Info, etc.).
Tap 'Audio Settings' from Device B's Device Settings screen.
Verify Audio Settings screen loads correctly showing options like ANC Level, Transparency Mode, EQ Presets, etc.
generated_testcases.expected: After deleting disconnected Device A, tapping Device B successfully opens Device Settings and Audio Settings without error, freeze, or redirect.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DELETE-004: Verify remaining device UI elements are intact after deleting a disconnected device (name, status, battery, settings gear)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
Pair and connect two devices: Device A and Device B.
Turn off Device A. Wait for 'Disconnected' status.
Delete Device A via device options.
Observe Device B's entry in the device list.
Verify Device B's name is fully visible and not truncated/corrupted.
Verify Device B's connection status shows 'Connected' with correct icon.
Verify Device B's battery level indicator (if applicable) is displayed correctly.
Verify the settings gear icon or menu button next to Device B is visible and tappable.
Tap the settings gear for Device B and confirm it opens Device B's settings.
generated_testcases.expected: Device B's UI entry in the device list displays all elements correctly: name, connection status, battery level, and tappable settings icon.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DELETE-005: Verify remaining device settings persist after app restart following device deletion
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
Pair and connect two devices: Device A and Device B.
Turn off Device A. Wait for 'Disconnected' status.
Delete Device A via device options.
Tap Device B to open Device Settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Transparency Mode → Environment.
Set Surround level to 75.
Navigate back to the main device list.
Force-close the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recent apps).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Verify only Device B appears in the device list (Device A should not reappear).
Tap Device B to open Device Settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Transparency Mode → Environment.
Verify Surround level is still set to 75.
generated_testcases.expected: After app restart, Device A is permanently removed, Device B appears correctly, and Device B's settings (Surround level = 75) persist.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DELETE-006: Verify deleting a disconnected device does not affect audio playback on the remaining connected device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
Pair and connect two devices: Device A and Device B.
Start playing music or a test tone on the phone, routed to Device B.
Verify audio is playing through Device B.
Turn off Device A. Wait for 'Disconnected' status.
Delete Device A via device options.
During and after deletion, verify audio continues to play through Device B without interruption, stutter, or drop.
Stop audio playback.
Restart audio playback and verify it still routes to Device B correctly.
generated_testcases.expected: Audio playback on the remaining connected Device B continues uninterrupted during and after deletion of the disconnected Device A.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DELETE-007: Verify deleting a disconnected device when remaining device is in a call does not disrupt the call
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
Pair and connect two devices: Device A and Device B.
Place or receive a phone call on Device B (connected).
Verify call audio is active through Device B.
Turn off Device A. Wait for 'Disconnected' status.
Delete Device A via device options.
During and after deletion, verify call audio on Device B remains clear and uninterrupted.
Verify call controls (mute, volume, end call) on Device B still function.
End the call and verify Device B returns to idle state correctly in the app.
generated_testcases.expected: An active call on the remaining connected Device B is not disrupted when the disconnected Device A is deleted from the app.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DELETE-008: Verify deleting a disconnected device and then reconnecting it re-pairs correctly without affecting the remaining device
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
Pair and connect two devices: Device A and Device B.
Turn off Device A. Wait for 'Disconnected' status.
Delete Device A via device options.
Verify only Device B remains in the device list with 'Connected' status.
Turn on Device A.
Put Device A into pairing mode (press Bluetooth button for 2 seconds until 'pairing' is heard).
Pair Device A with the phone via Bluetooth settings or the app's add device flow.
Verify Device A reappears in the Poly Studio Mobile app device list.
Verify Device B remains in the device list with 'Connected' status and correct name.
Verify both devices show correct connection statuses.
generated_testcases.expected: After deleting and re-pairing Device A, both devices appear correctly in the list with proper names and connection statuses, and Device B's settings remain intact.
